landing.description: Seamless access via NFC/Barcode. The interface adapts to your role. Own animals display full profiles; third-party tags show shared documents only.

docDetail.aiSelectProvider: Select the desired provider and model for document analysis.
docDetail.noProvidersConfigured: No AI providers configured. Please add an API key in your profile.
profile.aiPriorityDesc: The providers for automatic document analysis will be attempted in this order.
docScan.analyzeFailedRetry: Analysis failed. Document saved. Please try with a different model.
animal.analyzeFailedRetry: Error during analysis. Please try again later.
docs.title: Documentation
docs.user.title: End User Manual
docs.user.p1: Welcome! This system helps you digitally manage your animals and their documents.
docs.user.t1: Add animals via the 'Animals' tab. You can assign an NFC chip or barcode to each animal so it can be scanned globally if lost.
docs.user.t2: Navigate to the animal profile and click 'Scan Document'. Upload a photo. Our AI reads the document, categorizes it as vaccination or medication, and extracts all important data.
docs.user.t3: In the animal profile under 'Sharing' (lock icon), you decide exactly who can see which data. 'Read-only access' applies to public scans without login. 'Veterinarian' and 'Authority' for verified officials.
docs.admin.title: Administrator Manual
docs.admin.p1: The admin panel is only accessible to accounts with the 'admin' role.
docs.admin.t1: Vets can request verification. You approve these under 'Verifications'. Verified vets upload tamper-proof documents (with a green vet badge) which can no longer be deleted by the pet owner (see End User Manual).
docs.admin.t2: In the 'Settings' tab, you can change the app name, main color, and logo. These adjustments are instantly active globally for all users without needing a server restart.
docs.admin.t3: All critical actions (animal deleted, role changed, settings adjusted) are logged in a tamper-proof and traceable manner in the audit log.
docs.dev.title: Developer Documentation (API)
docs.dev.p1:
All REST API endpoints require a `Bearer <JWT>` token in the Authorization header (except Public routes).

The interactive Swagger/OpenAPI documentation can be found at the URL:
👉 /documentation
docs.dev.t1:
GET /api/animals (Own animals)
GET /api/animals/by-tag/:tagId (Find animal via NFC)
PATCH /api/animals/:id (Edit animal)
POST /api/documents/:id/retry-analysis (Restart AI analysis)
docs.dev.t3: The system supports Google Gemini (default), Anthropic Claude, and OpenAI. Users can store their own keys in the profile. The backend iterates through the providers based on `ai_provider_priority`.
